patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v4 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

version_history.subject: [PATCH v4 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 1/3] cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c
series_items.patch_summary: This patch moves the logic for managing persistent memory (pmem) regions from the overloaded region.c file into a new, dedicated region_pmem.c file. This separation makes it clear that this code only applies to pmem regions and helps to declutter the original file. No functional changes are made as part of this refactoring effort.

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM, and questioned whether the intention is to conditionally compile it based on this configuration option.
review_comments.raw_body:
This always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM.
Are you heading towards conditionally compiling, where region_pmem.o is
omitted if !CONFIG_CXL_PMEM? 

Similar question for region_dax.o

--Alison

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledges reviewer's concern about adding more Kconfig cruft, but doesn't commit to a fix; instead suggests that the current approach might be acceptable despite potential bloat for some deployments.
review_comments.raw_body:
I hadn't considered going that far, but it doesn't seem unreasonable?

It just seemed natural to break out the code this way, but I don't know
if adding more Kconfig cruft is worth it just to save some .o's and a
smaller amount of code.

The recent pmem/nvdimm Kconfig change does kind of show that we might
have some bloat for some deployments which might not care about pmem -
for example.

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Jonathan Cameron
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the cxl_pmem_region_release function is missing from region_pmem.c and requested it be added to properly release pmem regions

Reviewer Jonathan Cameron suggested that having region.o and region_pmem.o on one line in the Makefile makes it clearer that they are either both included or neither is, but noted that this would require more changes if the CONFIG_* definitions were later split up.

Reviewer noted that the lifetime management of cxl_pmem_region is more complex than desired, but did not provide specific technical details or suggestions for improvement.

Reviewer Jonathan Cameron noted that removing a blank line from the code could be unnecessary, as there was already one present in the original file and questioned its removal.

The reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a pmem region is deleted, and requested that the code be updated to properly clean up resources when a pmem region is removed.

Reviewer Jonathan Cameron expressed concern that the lifetime management of cxl_pmem_region is overly complex, but noted it's not a problem for this specific patch and couldn't immediately suggest improvements.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sun, 22 Mar 2026 09:16:36 -0400
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

---

cxl_core-$(CONFIG_CXL_REGION) += region.o region_pmem.o

For me having them on one line makes it more obvious it's
either neither or both. Cost is a bit more churn if you
decide later to split the CONFIG_* up.

---

Otherwise a couple of white space things. + a passing comment on the lifetime
management being more complex than I'd like.

Fix the white space and you can add
Reviewed-by: Jonathan Cameron <jonathan.cameron@huawei.com>

---

Blank line here. There is one in the original and I can't see why
we'd want to get rid of it.

---

Blank line here probably makes sense as the key has nothing to do
with the EXPORT_SYMBOL...

---

FWIW the lifetime management in here feels way to complex to me.  Not
a problem for this patch and I'm not immediately sure what we can do about it.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledges that device_add not being rolled back in other parts of the code is a common pattern, but does not explicitly state whether this is intentional or not.
review_comments.raw_body:
In many other parts of the code, device_add is not rolled back either
"for reasons", and - depending on who you talk to - this is
intentional... for debugging?

I dunno, but the "hanging objects on failure" thing is common throughout
cxl/ - :shrug:

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Jonathan Cameron
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the cxl_pmem_region_release function is not called when a pmem region device is removed, and suggested adding a release callback to the device_type structure.

The reviewer considers the removal of a specific cleanup path as a 'bug' because it is inconsistent with other paths in the function, even though it's unlikely to cause issues.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Mon, 23 Mar 2026 10:47:51 -0500
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

---

If it were consistent maybe, but in other paths in this function it is cleaned up...
So I call bug even if it's one that is relatively unlikely to actually happen.

J

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield
review_comments.summary: Reviewer expressed concerns about the long-term implications of moving pmem region driver logic into a separate file, specifically considering potential test complexity and missed tests if conditionally compiling is chosen in the future.
review_comments.raw_body:
I guess my original thought was, what is your long term intent with
the move?  And I was thinking you are probably heading to conditionally
compiling.  HOWEVER, now the scaredy-cat-me is thinking, shoot, that 
would add test config complexity and test misses. ie. Folks make a
change in region_dax.c, don't test with CONFIG_CXL_PMEM enabled, and
boom.

So, I lean towards keeping them all (working) together, like you have.

-- Alison

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author agrees that keeping the pmem region driver logic separate is a good approach to prevent additional Kconfig issues.
review_comments.raw_body:
fwiw i think this is the right take.  It doesn't really cost us anything
to keep in, and probably prevents adding more Kconfig footguns.

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield
review_comments.summary: Gave Reviewed-by

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 2/3] cxl/core/region: move dax region device logic into region_dax.c
series_items.patch_summary: This patch moves the CXL DAX region device logic from the overloaded core/region.c file into a new region_dax.c file, allowing for additional dax-driver integration paths without further dirtying the core logic. The move includes the necessary changes to Makefiles and header files, as well as the removal of redundant code in region.c. No functional changes are made by this patch.

review_comments.author: Jonathan Cameron
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the cxl_dax_region_release function is not being called when a device is removed, and suggested adding a call to devm_cxl_add_dax_region in the region_dax.c file to ensure proper cleanup.

Gave Reviewed-by
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sun, 22 Mar 2026 09:16:37 -0400
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield
review_comments.summary: Gave Reviewed-by

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 3/3] cxl/core: use cleanup.h for devm_cxl_add_dax_region
series_items.patch_summary: This patch series cleans up the cxl/core code by using cleanup.h for devm_cxl_add_dax_region, which involves refactoring the function to use a new cleanup mechanism. The changes are functional and do not introduce any new functionality.

review_comments.author: Jonathan Cameron
review_comments.summary: Applied his Reviewed-by tag without any comments or concerns.

>

Reviewed-by: Jonathan Cameron <jonathan.cameron@huawei.com>

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three new files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. The goal is to make it clear that certain logic applies only to specific types of regions, reducing development conflicts and improving code clarity.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [RFC PATCH 6/6] mm/memcontrol: Make memory.high tier-aware
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: Proposed patch to make memory.high tier-aware, but raised concerns about incorrect heuristics and potential OOM issues.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Identified incorrect heuristics in the proposed patch that could drive swap usage and invert LRU. Suggested revising the logic to manage top-tier memory for noisy-neighbor issues.

review_comments.author: Donet Tom
review_comments.summary: Expressed concern that demotion to lower tier under cgroup memory pressure could still trigger OOM, and asked if this is an issue to address.
review_comments.raw_body:
On 3/24/26 8:53 PM, Gregory Price wrote:
> On Tue, Mar 24, 2026 at 04:21:06PM +0530, Donet Tom wrote:
>> IIUC The intent of this patch is to partition cgroup memory such that
>> 0 \u2192 toptier_high is backed by higher-tier memory, and
>> toptier_high \u2192 max is backed by lower-tier memory.
>>
>> Based on this:
>>
>> 1.If top-tier usage exceeds toptier_high, pages should be
>>   demoted to the lower tier.
>>
>> 2. If lower-tier usage exceeds (max - toptier_high), pages
>>   should be swapped out.
>>
> This is not accurate and an incorrect heuristic.
>
> Transiently, lower-tier usage may exceed (max - toptier_high) for any
> number of reasons which should not be used as signal for pushing swap.
>
> driving swap usage is a function of (usage > memory.high) without regard
> for toptier / lowtier.
>
>> 3. If total memory usage exceeds max, demotion should be
>>   avoided and reclaim should directly swap out pages.
>>
> This is also incorrect, as it would drive agingin inversions.
> Demotion is a natural extension of the LRU infrastructure:
>
> toptier active -> toptier inactive -> lowtier inactive -> swap
>
> if you do (toptier inactive -> swap) you have inverted the LRU.


Thanks, Gregory, for the clarification.

One remaining concern is that under cgroup memory pressure,
demotion to the lower tier can still happen. Since demotion
does not uncharge the memcg, this could still trigger OOM.

Is this an issue we should address?


>
> As far as I know, from testing, we retain all the existing behavior - we
> are just managing a limited resource (top tier memory) to manage the
> noisy-neighbor issue.  So...
>
>
>> Should we also handle cases (2) and (3) in this patch?
> No, I don't think we should
>
> ~Gregory
>

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH v4 1/3] cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c
